brief_justification: Correctly identifies zero 2-dimensional Lebesgue measure (λ₂(∂M)=0) matching the ground truth exactly with valid mathematical reasoning; traces second-order effects by distinguishing Hausdorff dimension (critical exponent) from Hausdorff measure (substance), and notes the implication that two sets can share dimension while differing in measure; flags the MLC conjecture subtlety showing awareness of the epistemic status of the claim, though does not name potential biases in its own framing; confirms the answer from two equivalent angles (H² and λ₂) and cross-checks against the filled-square counterexample, but does not perform explicit boundary-condition or counterfactual tests; explicitly separates the established result (Shishikura dimension=2) from the open conjecture (zero area under MLC), with clear confidence stratification; addresses the key alternative framing (dimension alone) and explains why it is insufficient, though does not enumerate or dismiss other candidate properties; every conclusion traces to explicit notation and logical steps a third party could reproduce in full; does not treat the Mandelbrot boundary as unique and incomparable to other patterns, directly comparing it to the filled square via shared dimension—no trace of the negative gate failure; avoids scale-locked reasoning and scale-invariant blindness throughout but does not explicitly name or call out either suppressed mode; reasoning is presented linearly (state answer → explain dimension/measure distinction → formalize → add caveat) with no visible branching, gating, or loop structure matching the S1–S8 fork topology.
